title: Drip vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Drip vs Constant Contact: compare pricing, features, and real use cases to find the right email tool for your needs.
h1: Drip vs Constant Contact: Side-by-Side Comparison (2025)
intro: Choosing between Drip and Constant Contact comes down to your specific use case. Both are legitimate email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. Drip is built for e-commerce businesses wanting deep store integration, while Constant Contact targets nonprofits and local businesses. This comparison covers the key differences so you can decide quickly.

comparison_table.tool_a: E-Commerce Businesses Wanting Deep Store Integration

choose_a_if: You are primarily focused on e-commerce businesses wanting deep store integration
choose_b_if: You are primarily focused on nonprofits and local businesses

faq.q: Is Drip better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: It depends on your use case. Drip wins for e-commerce businesses wanting deep store integration, while Constant Contact is stronger for nonprofits and local businesses. Neither is objectively better — the right choice depends on your specific needs.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Drip or Constant Contact?
faq.a: Constant Contact starts at a lower price point ($12/mo vs $39/mo). However, pricing scales with subscriber count, so the total cost depends on your list size.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to Drip?
faq.a: Yes, switching from Constant Contact to Drip is possible. Most platforms support CSV import/export for subscriber lists. Plan for some time to recreate automations and templates, as these rarely transfer directly between platforms.
